annotations.label: Equivalence class (informal)

anchor.snippet: one may naturally split the set

annotations.note: `Setoid.classes r` is defined as the set of equivalence classes `{ s | ∃ y, s = { x | r x y } }`, exactly splitting α into classes.

annotations.label: Equivalence class (formal)

anchor.snippet: the equivalence class of an element

annotations.note: The equivalence class of `y` is `{ x | r x y }`, witnessed in Mathlib by `Setoid.mem_classes`.

annotations.label: Quotient set / quotient space

anchor.snippet: The set of the equivalence classes is sometimes called the quotient set

annotations.note: Mathlib has both `Quotient r` (core) and `Setoid.classes r`, with `Setoid.quotientEquivClasses` giving the canonical bijection.

annotations.label: Equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: An equivalence relation on a set

annotations.note: The Lean core `Setoid α` structure (with `iseqv : Equivalence r`) is the standard formalization, with `refl'`/`symm'`/`trans'` lemmas in `Mathlib.Data.Setoid.Basic`.

annotations.label: Equivalence class of an element

anchor.snippet: The equivalence class of an element

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `{ x | r x y } ∈ r.classes` via `Setoid.mem_classes`, formalizing `[a] = {x | x ~ a}`.

annotations.label: Isomorphism classes are proper classes

anchor.snippet: being isomorphic

annotations.note: Mathlib has no proper-class machinery; the meta-distinction between sets and proper classes for isomorphism collections is not formalized.

annotations.label: Quotient set and canonical surjection

anchor.snippet: The set of all equivalence classes

annotations.note: The canonical surjection `Quotient.mk` and its surjectivity are formalized in `Mathlib.Data.Quot` (`Quotient.mk_surjective`).

annotations.label: Representative and section

anchor.snippet: Every element of an equivalence class characterizes the class

annotations.note: `Quotient.out` is a (noncomputable) section choosing a representative of each class, satisfying `Quotient.mk_out`.

annotations.label: Canonical representatives (congruence mod m)

anchor.snippet: Sometimes, there is a section that is more

annotations.note: `ZMod n` represents classes by `Fin n` (canonical least-nonneg residue), but the general notion of "canonical representative section" is not abstracted.

annotations.label: Representative function (mod m remainder)

anchor.snippet: The use of representatives for representing classes

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `ZMod n`/`Int.emod` for the mod-m remainder representative, but no generic "representative-function-of-a-section" abstraction.

annotations.label: Equivalence classes partition the set

anchor.snippet: every element

annotations.note: `Setoid.isPartition_classes` states `r.classes` is a partition (`∅ ∉ classes ∧ ∀ a, ∃! b ∈ classes, a ∈ b`).

annotations.label: Partitions correspond to equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: every partition comes from an equivalence relation

annotations.note: `Setoid.Partition.orderIso` gives an order isomorphism between `Setoid α` and partitions of `α`.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations

anchor.snippet: the following statements are equivalent

annotations.note: `Quotient.eq` plus `Setoid.eq_of_mem_classes` formalize the equivalences `a ~ b ↔ ⟦a⟧ = ⟦b⟧ ↔ [a] ∩ [b] ≠ ∅`.

annotations.label: Rectangles with the same area

anchor.snippet: the set of all rectangles in a plane

annotations.note: This informal example about rectangles by area is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Modulo 2 and modulo 3 on integers

anchor.snippet: Consider the modulo 2 equivalence relation

annotations.note: `ZMod 2` and `ZMod 3` (and `Int.ModEq`) formalize integer congruence mod a natural number.

annotations.label: Construction of rational numbers

anchor.snippet: the set of ordered pairs of integers

annotations.note: `Rat` is formalized but as reduced fractions (numerator/denominator coprime), not as an explicit quotient of ℤ × ℤ\{0} by the cross-multiplication equivalence.

annotations.label: Parallel lines and points at infinity

anchor.snippet: all the lines in the Euclidean plane

annotations.note: `Projectivization` formalizes projective space as a quotient by linear-span equivalence, generalizing the parallel-lines-with-points-at-infinity example.

annotations.label: Equality relation

anchor.snippet: every equivalence class is a set consisting of a single element

annotations.note: `Setoid.bot_def` identifies `(⊥ : Setoid α)` with equality `(· = ·)`, whose classes are singletons.

annotations.label: Cluster graphs of equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: An undirected graph may be associated to any symmetric relation

annotations.note: The cluster-graph viewpoint relating equivalence relations to disjoint unions of complete graphs is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant / well-defined under a relation

anchor.snippet: is said to be an invariant

annotations.note: `Quotient.lift f h` requires the hypothesis `∀ a b, a ≈ b → f a = f b`, exactly the invariance/well-definedness condition.

annotations.label: Class invariant function

anchor.snippet: A frequent particular case occurs

annotations.note: `Quotient.lift` realizes a function that is constant on each class as a function on the quotient.

annotations.label: Kernel of a function as equivalence relation

anchor.snippet: Any function

annotations.note: `Setoid.ker f` is the equivalence relation `f x = f y` induced by `f`.

annotations.label: Morphism of sets with equivalence relations

anchor.snippet: More generally, a function may map equivalent arguments

annotations.note: `Quotient.map f h` lifts a relation-preserving function `f : α → β` to a function `Quotient sa → Quotient sb`.

annotations.label: Quotient space in topology

anchor.snippet: a quotient space is a topological space

annotations.note: `instTopologicalSpaceQuotient` endows `Quotient s` with the quotient topology from a topological space `X`.

annotations.label: Quotient algebra and quotient space (algebra)

anchor.snippet: congruence relations on the underlying set of an algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib has many quotient algebraic structures (e.g. `QuotientGroup`, `Ideal.Quotient`, `Submodule.Quotient`) built from congruence relations, though there is no single "quotient algebra" abstraction.

annotations.label: Quotient space of a group action

anchor.snippet: The orbits of a group action

annotations.note: `MulAction.orbitRel G α` is the orbit equivalence, and `Quotient (MulAction.orbitRel G α)` is the orbit space.

annotations.label: Normal subgroup of a topological group

anchor.snippet: A normal subgroup of a topological group

annotations.note: `QuotientGroup.instIsTopologicalGroup` shows `G ⧸ N` is a topological group when `G` is a topological group and `N` is a normal subgroup.
